objective: Add one dependency-free bounded accepted-data contract for the eleven volatile stream-info roles observed by LSLC-001H, preserving caller-owned opaque text unchanged while distinguishing implementation-assigned version data, runtime-assigned creation/identity/session/host data, and transport-owned address/port data, without adding provider acquisition, XML or document representation, clocks, host inspection, identity generation, address or port interpretation, networking, runtime activation, or Manifold authority.
outputs: A fixed eleven-role data inventory in accepted LSLC-001H order with explicit implementation-assigned, runtime-assigned, and transport-owned classifications.
One unvalidated owning input and one privately accepted value retaining all eleven caller-provided Strings unchanged under three explicit nonzero Unicode scalar-value maxima.
Deterministic limit validation in classification order and field validation in fixed role order, with typed errors retaining the exact failed class or role and expected/actual counts.

Focused positive and damaged evidence that binds accepted public role observations, proves opaque preservation and boundary closure, and reconciles stale public status prose inside the declared instruction update.

acceptance.proof: The contract validates three nonzero scalar-value maxima, checks all eleven fields in fixed observed role order, returns typed deterministic errors, and accepts only complete caller-owned input without allocation, clone, normalization, parsing, or inference.

The exact version, created_at, uid, session_id, hostname, v4address, v4data_port, v4service_port, v6address, v6data_port, and v6service_port order is bound to accepted LSLC-001H evidence, while implementation-assigned, runtime-assigned, and transport-owned classifications remain explicit and disjoint.
Empty, Unicode, delimiter-sensitive, and deliberately non-numeric/non-address text remains unchanged, and consuming access returns the original eleven String allocations in role order.

The implementation and evidence add no provider trait or acquisition, clock or host read, generated identity, XML validation or representation, address/port semantics, transport behavior, runtime activation, device behavior, or Manifold authority.
Cargo files, dependencies, feature lock, effect union, accepted oracle fixtures and drivers, external-source boundary, devices, and runtime surfaces remain unchanged and inert.

Public instructions and status prose accurately route the accepted observation, static/description composition, and new volatile data layer while preserving distinct specification, observation, candidate-data, representation, provider, and authority roles.

commit_policy: Commit the bounded volatile accepted-data contract separately from XML or complete-document representation, providers, clocks, host inspection, generated identity, address or port interpretation, transport, discovery, adapters, runtime activation, features, devices, or external integration.

instruction_surfaces.change_reason: Route the eleven-role bounded data contract, its three ownership classes, validation order, and strict provider/representation/runtime/authority boundary.
Describe the new volatile data layer and reconcile stale status prose that predates accepted LSLC-001H through LSLC-001N evidence and implementations.
Review whether the explicit data/provider/representation and ownership split adds reusable guidance; retain unchanged if existing artifact-role and authority rules cover it.
instruction_surfaces.validation: Review the installed skill and run its instruction-surface whitespace check.

instruction_surfaces.change_reason: Review whether this source-only Rusty LSL contract changes repo-family routing; retain unchanged if current public-source, activation, and Manifold boundaries cover it.
instruction_surfaces.validation: Review the installed skill and run its instruction-surface whitespace check.

non_scope: Any change to Cargo.toml, Cargo.lock, project or feature locks, accepted fixtures, oracle drivers, dependencies, Cargo features, unsafe Rust, generated protocol material, native libraries, or implementation-source inputs.

XML names, legal-character validation, escaping, tree construction, serialization, declaration, whitespace, empty-element spelling, complete-document composition, parsing, decoding, canonicalization, endpoint bytes, or compatibility result promotion.

Provider traits or selection, acquisition callbacks, clock reads, time calculation, host or environment inspection, random or generated identifiers, address-family validation, address parsing, port parsing or range checks, socket ownership, networking, discovery, inlet, outlet, transport, buffering, recovery, FFI, adapters, or runtime activation.
Interpreting the eleven opaque values as protocol, wire, endpoint, globally unique identity, security, recovery, authorization, routing, permission, admission, or accepted runtime facts.
Oracle execution, network access, external implementation-source inspection, devices, Quest, Hostess, platform lifecycle, product policy, or operational/ecosystem compatibility claims.
Manifold admission, registry, subscription, route, lease, revision, replay, identity, authorization, audit, command, session, topology, transport, provider epoch, or product-policy authority.
